message:
feat(google-docs): verify capability_token against declared operation capability on invoke

Brings google-docs in line with the capability-enforcement contract
that every other connector in the tree now honors: each invocation
proves that the presented capability_token was granted for the
capability this operation declares.

Changes in `DocsConnector::handle_invoke`:

- Require `params["capability_token"]` on every call; return
  `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1001, message: "Missing
  'capability_token' field" }` when it is absent, and the same shape
  with "Invalid capability_token format: {e}" when serde fails to
  deserialize it into `CapabilityToken`.
- Parse the incoming `operation: &str` into an `OperationId` and
  return `InvalidRequest { code: 1002, message: "Invalid operation
  ID: {operation}" }` on parse failure.
- Call `self.handle_introspect().await?` to recover the canonical
  operations table, then look up the entry whose `id` equals the
  current operation and pull its declared `capability` string. If
  the operation is not declared at all, return
  `OperationNotGranted { operation }` — this enforces the invariant
  that ad-hoc operations cannot be invoked even if the caller
  somehow gets past the handshake.
- Parse that declared capability into a `CapabilityId` and, when
  `self.verifier` is configured, call
  `verifier.verify(token, &cap_id, &op_id, &[])`. When there is no
  verifier, refuse with `FcpError::NotConfigured` — fail closed.
- The downstream `let client = self.client.as_ref().ok_or(...)` path
  and the `match operation` dispatch table are unchanged.

Imports add `CapabilityToken` to the `fcp_core::{...}` use-list;
`CapabilityVerifier`, `CapabilityGrant`, `CapabilityId`, and the rest
were already imported.

This closes a capability-bypass window where a client that knew a
valid handshake could invoke any Docs operation without the host
ever inspecting the token's grants — matching the behavior that
`verify_issued_token_wrong_connector_fails_scope` (in the fcp-host
admin-state tests committed alongside) asserts at the host layer.
